summary: We recommend logging the IDLE hold-pattern observation as a tick artifact rather than letting the heartbeat fire without producing a record because the operator-only stop rule forbids the orchestrator from calling CronDelete and the loop-prompt-suggested stop-on-idle path conflicts with that rule. The tradeoff against shipping a trivial IDLE PR is that it creates one row of tick-log evidence per holding period which lets future operators audit that the heartbeat understood the hold-pattern state correctly. PR opensubagents outcomes-mcp 18 the B5 floor ratchet is mergeable with five green checks and auto-merge intentionally null awaiting the operator's explicit merge decision per the B5 queue row text and ADR 0001's deferral note.

claims.statement: QUEUE now records 25 done 1 held 2 blocked and 0 pending unblocked because the queue table shows A1 through A5 done B1 through B4 done B5 in_progress C1 C2 C4 C5 C7 done C3 and C6 blocked D1 through D6 done E1 through E4 done F1 through F4 done.

citations.title: QUEUE md current status across all 31 outcomes
RESUME md state-at-a-glance section that records the same counts

claims.caveats: B5 is technically in_progress not pending because tick 17 advanced it to the held-for-merge state; the 1-held count above treats in_progress-awaiting-human as a distinct bucket from in_progress-awaiting-CI.

claims.statement: PR opensubagents outcomes-mcp 18 the B5 floor ratchet remains open with five CI checks all passing Analyze actions Analyze javascript-typescript CodeQL outcome-gate and typecheck and auto-merge intentionally null because the heartbeat respects the B5 queue row text hold for human merge and the operator owns the merge decision.

citations.title: outcomes-mcp pull 18 B5 ratchet with all checks green and auto-merge unset
ADR 0001 Decision section 4 defers the ratchet pending calibration shoulder pairs

claims.caveats: Operator may decide to merge B5 immediately on the basis that the matrix at floor 4.0 is also perfect or may defer until naturalistic shoulder pairs exist; either decision is in scope and the heartbeat will record whichever path the operator chooses on the next tick.

claims.statement: the heartbeat does not call CronDelete or skip the next tick fire because the operator-only stop rule documented in the loop-prompt and the orchestrator memory file overrides the loop-prompt's own stop-on-idle suggestion when those conflict.

citations.title: loop-prompt md rule never stop the loop operator stops it via Esc
RESUME md What's next section describes the hold-pattern recommendation

claims.caveats: Future ticks while the hold persists will keep producing IDLE artifacts which is verbose but is the correct trade against silent degradation; an operator who finds the verbosity excessive can press Esc to stop the loop.

open_questions: Should the heartbeat consolidate consecutive IDLE ticks into a single rolling artifact rather than producing one PR per IDLE tick to reduce tick-log noise once the hold has lasted more than a few ticks?
methodology_notes: Re-read QUEUE md re-checked PR opensubagents outcomes-mcp 18 status and CI conclusions then confirmed every other QUEUE row is in a terminal state. Authored the IDLE artifact to record the observation rather than silently no-op.
